title: U.S. Gas Prices Hit $4 a Gallon on Average
summary: A month since the first U.S.-Israeli attacks and Iran’s response effectively shut off Persian Gulf oil, drivers are paying significantly more to fill up.

title: Drivers Count Their Pennies as Gas Hits $4 a Gallon Because of War in Iran
summary: Since the Iran conflict began on Feb. 28, gas prices across the United States have increased about 35 percent. They are now above $4 a gallon, and drivers are wincing.

title: Will $4 Gas Hurt Trump’s Approval Ratings? Here’s What History Shows.
summary: Presidents since at least the Carter administration have seen their approval ratings tied to gas prices. But there are signs the correlation may be weakening.

title: Fresh Food Distributors Add Surcharges as Fuel Costs Rise
summary: Delivering salmon, fruits and other perishable foods has become more expensive as the war with Iran pushes up diesel prices.

title: Trump Faces a Decision on Whether to Start a Ground War in Iran
summary: The president wants a negotiation, but the Iranians say they are refusing until a cease-fire is declared. And while Marines and the 82nd Airborne Division offer new leverage, the risks escalate quickly.

title: Europe Has a ‘Guns vs. Butter’ Problem. War in Iran Makes It Worse.
summary: After decades of prioritizing domestic over military spending, the continent’s leaders are trying to pivot. That is straining national budgets and could anger voters.

title: Offensive vs. Defensive: On Iran, the Distinction Matters for Britain’s Leader
summary: As the United States expands its armada of warplanes on British soil, Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he is trying to defend Britain’s interests while keeping the country out of war.

title: Deaths in ICE Custody Are Growing, and Trump Defends Ballroom Plans
summary: Plus, a three-minute, multimillion-dollar art heist.

title: Putin’s Internet Blackout: A Chaotic Drive to Cut Off Russians From the World
summary: With new outages and blockages, President Vladimir V. Putin is taking his boldest steps yet to control Russians’ communications.

title: Inside Russia During an Internet Crackdown
summary: Our international correspondent Valerie Hopkins walks us through how she connects to the internet in Russia as the Kremlin clamps down and Moscow experiences major internet blackouts.

title: A Cat-and-Mouse Game of Russian Internet Restrictions and Evasion
summary: As the Kremlin spends heavily on censorship technology, Russians are scrambling to find new ways to circumvent the limits.

title: ‘The Book of Mormon’ Is Sorry if You Have Been Offended for 15 Years
summary: The taboo-busting, gasp-inducing Broadway musical comedy has been a hit with audiences and critics. But could it be produced today?

title: In a Moment of Division, the Astronauts on the Artemis II Mission Hope to Inspire
summary: Can the four astronauts of the NASA mission Artemis II make a difference in a distracted and divided world?

title: For Trump, the Artemis II Moon Mission Offers a Shot to Cement His Legacy
summary: No president since the Apollo era has pushed harder to return to the moon than President Trump. But he wants a space achievement that is about “more than getting rocks this time.”

title: First Canadian Astronaut Will Travel to the Moon Amid Fraying U.S.-Canada Relations
summary: Canada will send its first astronaut to the moon on a joint mission with the United States, but back on Earth, the relationship between the two countries is fraying.

title: After Months of Threats, Trump Softens His Stance on Blocking Oil to Cuba
summary: The White House press secretary said the United States would evaluate oil shipments to Cuba on a “case-by-case basis,” after a Russian tanker full of crude reached the island.

title: Edward Russo Says Donald Trump Is an ‘Environmental Hero’
summary: Edward Russo, the only member of a White House task force, thinks the president doesn’t get enough credit for conservation at his golf courses, among other things.

title: Trump Reveals New Look for White House Ballroom After Times Article
summary: Architects and preservation organizations have warned that little scrutiny has been given to the project’s design plans.

title: DeSantis Signs Bill to Rename Florida Airport for Trump
summary: The airport near West Palm Beach will be rebranded the President Donald J. Trump International Airport on July 1, if approved by the Federal Aviation Administration.

title: The Push to Ban Cars From All New York City Parks
summary: Central Park is car-free, and so is Prospect Park. What about the rest?

title: Inflation Hit Iceland Hard. Even Its Beloved Hot Dogs.
summary: This reliable food has long been a cheap option in an expensive country. Steadily increasing prices have locals complaining, but they can’t stop ordering one with everything.

title: The People Trump Pardoned Are on a Crime Spree
summary: The president and the Republican Party deserve to pay a political price for the pardons.

title: The Greatest Threat to Our Elections Is No Longer Foreign
summary: This is not how American democracy is supposed to work.

title: A Lifeline for Cuba
summary: We look at the energy crisis on the Caribbean island.

title: Stephen Colbert Marvels at ‘No Kings’ Rallies Worldwide
summary: “Just look at the crowd we got here in New York,” he said. “Oop, that’s the T.S.A. line at J.F.K.”

title: When Will Japan’s Cherry Blossoms Bloom? A.I. Can Help Answer That
summary: Experts use artificial intelligence to analyze data, plus thousands of crowdsourced photos, to forecast the prized flowers, which are a multibillion-dollar attraction.

title: Why Barbie Dream Fest in Florida Is Drawing Comparisons to Fyre Festival
summary: Fans had high hopes for the event, a Mattel-sanctioned gathering in a Florida convention center. Now, it’s drawing comparisons to Fyre Festival.

title: The Army Begins an Inquiry After Kid Rock Gets Apache Helicopter Flyby
summary: The pro-Trump musician saluted the pair of Apache attack choppers, which appeared to be the same ones that flew low over a “No Kings” rally in Nashville.

title: These Two Countries With Social Media Bans Aren’t Happy With Google and Meta
summary: Indonesia says the two companies are violating its law that bars children under 16 from the social media. Australia has similar concerns.
